content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 3, 1989 
The Honorable Paul Carpenter 
Fourth District Member 
state Board of Equalization 
4040 Paramount Blvd., suite 103 
Lakewood, CA 
90712 
Dear Mr. Carpenter: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-372 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act of the 
1974.1/ 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
How long must campaign committee records be retained 
for an active committee? 
2. 
How long must campaign committee records be retained 
for a terminated committee? 
CONCLUSION 
Committee campaign records must be retained for four 
years following the date that the campaign statement to which 
they relate is filed. 
As an example, if a state senator was elected or re-
elected in November of 1984, all campaign records relating to 
the post election statement filed January 31, 1985, may be 
discarded February 1, 1989. 
Whether a candidate's committee is active or terminated 
is not relevant. 
1/ 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et ale 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Honorable Paul Carpenter 
February 3, 1989 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
Each candidate, treasurer and elected officer is 
required to maintain detailed accounts, records, bills and 
receipts necessary to prepare campaign statements, and to 
retain these accounts, records, bills and receipts for a 
period of time specified by the Commission. 
(Section 84104.) 
Regulation 18401(b} specifies the length of time 
campaign records shall be retained: 
The documents required by sUbsection (a) shall be 
retained by the filer for a period of four years 
following the date that the campaign statement to which 
they relate is filed. 
However, in the case of an 
elected state officer serving a four year term, the 
records for campaign statements filed during the first 
year following their election must be kept for five 
years following the date such statements are filed. 
The Act does not differentiate between an active 
committee and a terminated committee concerning retention of 
records. 
Enclosed for your information is Regulation 18401. 
If 
you have additional questions, please call me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Co~nsel ~ 
/ 
.~ 
U~t0 r 
By: 
Braaten-Moen 
~itical Reform Consultant

the

of 
campaign committee records, and I have two specific questions: 
1. How long must records be kept for an active committee? 
2. How long must records be kept for a terminated committee? 
I would appreciate your written response at your convenience. 
Sincerely, 
i

Paul Carpenter

Executive Director 
Paul Carpenter

January 20, 1989 
Fair political Practices Commission 
428 J street, suite 800

807 
Sacramento, Ca 
95804 
Dear Mr. Baugher: 
Sacramento Office 
1020 N Street 
Room 125 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
(916) 445-4664 
Several months ago, I wrote the enclosed letter regarding the storage 
of campaign committee records. 
Despite Ms. Pritchard's letter of September 29, 1988, and follow-up 
telephone calls, I have yet to receive an answer to my question. 
Therefore, I would very much appreciate your attention to this matter 
to insure that I am provided with a reply in the near future. 
PC:apl 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 


~

I would appreciate your written response at your convenience. 
Sincerely,
